    Home › Manufacturers › Hughes

    Hughes

    United States · Founded 1932

    One billionaire, one wooden giant, one 26-second flight.

    Aircraft here1
    Longest66.65 m
    Combined built1
    First flights1947–1947
    The company

    The story so far

    Howard Hughes built aircraft the way he did everything else — enormously, secretively, and on his own terms. His H-4 Hercules was the largest aircraft in the world, built from laminated birch because wartime rules reserved aluminium for other programmes.

    It flew once, for about 26 seconds, in November 1947, with Hughes himself at the controls. Its 97.54-metre wingspan stood as the widest of any aircraft ever flown for seventy-two years — a record broken only in 2019.

    The line-up

    Hughes aircraft

    Silhouettes are sized by real wingspan — bigger aircraft draw bigger.

    Grid

    Hughes H-4 Hercules

    Hughes · Preserved — flew once, 1947

    Length66.65 m
    Wingspan97.54 m
    Height24.18 m
    MTOW180,000 kg

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
